tripOverview.intro: London captivates couples with its perfect blend of historic charm and modern romance. From intimate canal walks to world-class museums, the city offers endless romantic discoveries.
tripOverview.vibe: A whirlwind romantic adventure through London's most charming neighborhoods, perfect for couples who love culture, great food, and LGBTQ+-friendly spaces.

destinationOverview.bestTime: April is perfect for London with mild spring weather and blooming flowers throughout the city's parks.

attractions.name: Tower of London
attractions.desc: Historic fortress housing the Crown Jewels and centuries of royal history.

attractions.name: British Museum
attractions.desc: World-renowned museum with artifacts from ancient civilizations across the globe.

attractions.name: Borough Market
attractions.desc: London's oldest food market offering artisanal products and international cuisine.

attractions.name: Tate Modern
attractions.desc: Contemporary art gallery housed in a former power station on the Thames.

destinationOverview.transportation: London's extensive public transport system includes the Underground (Tube), buses, and overground trains connecting all areas efficiently. Black cabs are iconic but pricier, while Uber and Bolt offer convenient rideshare options. An Oyster Card or contactless payment provides the best value for multiple journeys across all transport modes.
destinationOverview.tip: Always stand right on escalators and have your Oyster Card or phone ready for quick tap-in access - Londoners move fast during rush hours!
destinationOverview.safety: London is very safe for LGBTQ+ travelers with excellent police presence and well-lit streets throughout central areas.
destinationOverview.airaloText: Stay connected with affordable eSIM data plans perfect for navigating London's streets and sharing your romantic moments instantly.

days.intro: Start your London adventure with iconic sights and royal history.

morning.name: Tower of London
morning.desc: Explore the historic fortress and see the dazzling Crown Jewels.

afternoon.name: Tower Bridge
afternoon.desc: Walk across London's most famous bridge with stunning Thames views.

afternoon.name: Thames River Walk
afternoon.desc: Romantic stroll along the South Bank towards London Bridge.

evening.name: Dinner at Dishoom
evening.desc: Enjoy authentic Indian cuisine in this beloved Bombay-style café.

days.tip: Book Tower of London tickets online to skip the queues, especially during spring when tourist numbers increase.

days.intro: Discover London's incredible food scene and world-class art collections.

morning.name: Borough Market
morning.desc: Explore London's most famous food market with amazing vegetarian options.

afternoon.name: Tate Modern
afternoon.desc: Browse contemporary art in this spectacular riverside gallery.

afternoon.name: Millennium Bridge
afternoon.desc: Cross the Thames on this pedestrian bridge to St. Paul's area.

evening.name: Drinks at The George Inn
evening.desc: Historic pub with cozy atmosphere perfect for couples.

days.tip: Borough Market gets very busy after 11 AM, so arrive early for the best selection and easier navigation.

days.intro: Enjoy London's green spaces and dive into its incredible museum collections.

morning.name: British Museum
morning.desc: Marvel at world treasures from ancient Egypt to Greek sculptures.

afternoon.name: Regent's Park
afternoon.desc: Romantic walk through beautiful gardens and rose displays.

afternoon.name: Camden Market
afternoon.desc: Browse unique shops and international street food vendors.

evening.name: Dinner at Mildreds
evening.desc: Celebrated vegetarian restaurant with creative plant-based dishes.

days.tip: The British Museum is huge - focus on 2-3 galleries that interest you most rather than trying to see everything.

days.intro: Savor your last morning with London's best coffee and shopping before departure.

morning.name: Covent Garden
morning.desc: Browse shops and enjoy street performers in this historic market.

morning.name: Coffee at Workshop Coffee
morning.desc: London's finest specialty coffee before your journey home.

afternoon.name: Last-minute shopping
afternoon.desc: Pick up souvenirs and British treats to take home.

afternoon.name: Airport departure
afternoon.desc: Head to Heathrow for your flight back to Philadelphia.

days.tip: Allow 3 hours for international departures from Heathrow and check in online to save time at the airport.

food.intro: London's food scene brilliantly caters to vegetarians with incredible Indian restaurants and innovative plant-based cuisine.
food.tip: Many restaurants offer pre-theatre menus with excellent value, and always check if service charge is included in your bill.

neighborhoods.name: Southwark
neighborhoods.desc: Near Borough Market and Tate Modern with great riverside walks
neighborhoods.whyStay: Walking distance to your must-see Borough Market

neighborhoods.name: Covent Garden
neighborhoods.desc: Central location with theaters, shops, and restaurants
neighborhoods.whyStay: Heart of London with everything at your doorstep

neighborhoods.name: Shoreditch
neighborhoods.desc: Trendy area with vibrant LGBTQ+ scene and creative culture
neighborhoods.whyStay: Hip neighborhood with inclusive venues and great restaurants

budget.name: Premier Inn London Southwark
budget.vibe: Clean and comfortable with great location

budget.name: YHA London Central
budget.vibe: Modern hostel with private rooms available

midRange.name: The Z Hotel Piccadilly
midRange.vibe: Stylish boutique hotel in prime location

midRange.name: Point A Hotel London Shoreditch
midRange.vibe: Modern design in trendy LGBTQ+-friendly area

luxury.name: The Shard Hotel
luxury.vibe: Sky-high luxury with incredible city views

luxury.name: Mondrian London
luxury.vibe: Thames-side luxury with stylish river views

hotels.tip: Book accommodations near tube stations for easy access to all attractions, and many hotels offer early check-in if you arrive on morning flights.

getYourGuide.name: London Food Walking Tour
getYourGuide.desc: Discover the city's best vegetarian and international cuisine with a local guide.

getYourGuide.name: Skip-the-Line Tower of London
getYourGuide.desc: Fast-track entry to see the Crown Jewels and learn about royal history.

getYourGuide.name: Thames Evening Cruise
getYourGuide.desc: Romantic sunset cruise along the Thames with views of illuminated landmarks.

hiddenGems.name: Leadenhall Market
hiddenGems.desc: Beautiful Victorian market featured in Harry Potter films with unique shops.

hiddenGems.name: Kyoto Garden
hiddenGems.desc: Peaceful Japanese garden hidden within Holland Park, perfect for quiet moments.

hiddenGems.name: God's Own Junkyard
hiddenGems.desc: Neon wonderland gallery showcasing vintage signs and electric art installations.

packing.title: April in London
packing.intro: Pack layers for London's mild spring weather with occasional rain showers and cool evenings.
packing.luggage: A carry-on suitcase should suffice for this short trip, but check your airline's specific size requirements.
packing.tip: Pack an extra layer in your carry-on as London weather can be unpredictable, and wear your heaviest shoes on the plane to save luggage space.

bookTrip.intro: Round-trip flights from Philadelphia to London typically cost $650-850 per person in April.
